title: tabernaemontana africana
intro: Tabernaemontana africana, also known as the African yellow wood or yellow poison, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which contribute to its various health benefits such as reducing pain, fever, and inflammation. The health benefits of Tabernaemontana africana include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy in African traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and ointments for various health purposes.
